calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Dec-29-2014 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Dec-10-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. CAROLINE MCCORMICK, TEL. NO. (925)628-4470 SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF UNIFUND CCR LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is Granted without leave to amend. No opposition filed. Judgment in favor of plaintiff to be entered forthwith. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098, TEL. NO. (415)717-9768 =302/ehg

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Nov-14-2014 Continued To Dec-10-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF UNIFUND CCR LLC'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is continued to December 10, 2014 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302 to give the moving party an opportunity to (1) attach a valid proof of service and to (2) provide the Court with the documents for which the party seeks judicial notice. The moving party must deliver these papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than November 14, 2014 at 4:00 p.m. or the hearing will be taken off calendar. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-08-2014 Set For Trial On Jan-20-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order To Deem Matters Admitted
Off Calendar Jun-25-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: PLAINTIFF UNIFUND CCR LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order To Deem Matters Admitted is Granted, unless within ten (10) days from the date of this order Plaintiff files full and complete responses to the request for admission. Failure to do so will preclude from challenging the truth of the requested admission or the genuiness of any of the documents referenced therein. Order granting Plaintiff's motion for order to deem matters admitted is signed in Open Court.
Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
